_meta.note: Daemon-backed session line execution (omni P1). The line runs in the session's sticky cwd/env (set by prior lines such as `cd /tmp`). The daemon writes the line to the persistent shell and returns the combed signals it produced (read from the session bucket via a bounded wait), plus the next cursor for the following exec. Never a raw screen buffer or stdout dump. TB-7: session_id is opaque (copy verbatim, e.g. ses_<32hex>; from shell_session_start).

unsupported_platform_error_example.message: daemon ipc error [UnsupportedPlatform]: persistent shell sessions are not available on this platform yet (PTY/ConPTY pending)

session_not_live_error_example.message: daemon ipc error [SessionNotLive]: session ses_018f0000000000000000000000000003 is not live (state observed terminal)

invariants: session_id and line are required; the daemon appends the trailing newline (do NOT include one)
a send to a non-live session fails loudly with SessionNotLive, never hangs (terminal-state guard)
the line bytes are never echoed in the response; only the combed signals it produced are returned
success payload reports combed events + the next cursor; never raw stdout/stderr
while a secret prompt is active on the session shell, the write is denied (SecretInputDenied)
